varieties.name: Common Spearmint (Garden Mint) 🏆
varieties.origin: Europe and Asia, cultivated for centuries
varieties.traits: Dark green serrated leaves with crinkled texture, grows 30-60cm tall, square stems
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive, can be harvested multiple times per season
varieties.availability: Widely available from Queensland nurseries including Basilea Living Herbs (humid subtropical climates), Bunnings, local garden centers

varieties.name: Kentucky Colonel Spearmint 🏆
varieties.origin: United States, Kentucky region, heirloom cultivar
varieties.traits: Large, broad, pale green wrinkled leaves, upright growth 30-60cm, robust stems
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, slower-spreading than common spearmint
varieties.availability: Specialty herb nurseries, online suppliers, sometimes Bunnings seasonal stock

varieties.name: Curly Spearmint (Mentha spicata var. crispa)
varieties.traits: Distinctive curled, ruffled leaves, compact growth, ornamental appearance
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good, decorative and functional
varieties.availability: Select nurseries, online herb specialists

varieties.name: Moroccan Mint (Nana Mint)
varieties.traits: Bright green pointed leaves, compact habit, intense aroma
varieties.availability: Specialty herb nurseries, Mediterranean herb suppliers

varieties.name: Tashkent Mint
varieties.traits: Similar appearance to spearmint, vigorous growth
varieties.availability: Limited availability, specialist collectors

varieties.name: Common Peppermint 🏆
varieties.origin: Natural hybrid (M. aquatica × M. spicata), Europe, 1700s
varieties.traits: Dark green pointed leaves with purple-tinged stems, grows 60-90cm, spreading habit
varieties.availability: Widely available throughout Queensland - Bunnings, nurseries, garden centers

varieties.name: Chocolate Mint
varieties.traits: Dark green leaves with brown-purple stems and veins, 30-60cm tall, attractive purple flowers
varieties.availability: Common at Basilea Living Herbs, Bunnings, local nurseries

varieties.name: Orange Mint (Bergamot Mint)
varieties.traits: Smooth green leaves with reddish stems, spreading growth 40-60cm
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, select garden centers

varieties.name: Lavender Mint
varieties.traits: Purple-tinged leaves and reddish stems, lavender flowers
varieties.availability: Limited availability, specialty herb nurseries

varieties.name: Grapefruit Mint
varieties.traits: Short stems with large downy leaves, attractive appearance
varieties.availability: Rare, specialty collectors

varieties.name: Black Peppermint
varieties.traits: Dark purple-black stems, green leaves with purple undertones
varieties.availability: Limited commercial availability

varieties.name: Common Apple Mint (Woolly Mint) 🏆
varieties.origin: Southern and Western Europe, Mediterranean region
varieties.traits: Light green, round, hairy leaves with soft fuzzy texture, stiff stems 50-90cm tall, white-pink flowers
varieties.yield_notes: Productive, less aggressive spreader than other mints
varieties.availability: Available from Queensland herb nurseries, garden centers

varieties.name: Pineapple Mint (Variegated Apple Mint)
varieties.traits: Beautiful cream and pale green variegated fuzzy leaves, white or mauve flowers, 60-90cm tall, ornamental
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, grown as much for ornamental value
varieties.availability: Common at Basilea Living Herbs, Bunnings, local nurseries

varieties.name: Golden Apple Mint
varieties.traits: Deep green leaves variegated with yellow, spicy apple fragrance
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Strawberry Mint
varieties.traits: Small delicate leaves, compact growth 30-60cm, good for containers
varieties.availability: Specialty herb suppliers, online nurseries

varieties.name: Banana Mint
varieties.traits: Small light green leaves, low-growing creeping habit, 30-45cm tall
varieties.availability: Specialty collectors, rare

varieties.name: Lime Mint
varieties.traits: Green leaves with citrus scent, similar growth to peppermint
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Ginger Mint (Red Mint, Scotch Spearmint) 🏆
varieties.origin: Hybrid (M. × gracilis = M. arvensis × M. spicata)
varieties.traits: Small green leaves with yellow variegation, red upright stems 45cm tall, slightly fuzzy
varieties.availability: Specialty herb nurseries, Portland Nursery style suppliers

varieties.name: Doublemint (Madalene Hill)
varieties.origin: Texas, USA - developed by herbalist Madalene Hill (M. × gracilis)
varieties.traits: Red stems, green leaves, upright growth
varieties.availability: Specialty herb collectors, rare in Australia

varieties.name: Mojito Mint (Cuban Mint)
varieties.traits: Large leaves, spearmint-like appearance, vigorous growth 60cm
varieties.availability: Increasingly available at specialty nurseries, Basilea Living Herbs

varieties.name: Bowles Mint
varieties.traits: Large fuzzy leaves, robust growth, rounded appearance
varieties.availability: Specialist herb nurseries

varieties.name: Native River Mint (Wild Mint) 🏆 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to Australia, found across most regions including Queensland
varieties.traits: Smaller, more delicate than European mints, green leaves, 30cm high, 100cm spread, purple-white flowers
varieties.availability: Tucker Bush (Australian native specialist), native plant nurseries Queensland

varieties.name: Bush Mint (Native Pennyroyal) 🏆 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to Queensland, NSW, Victoria, SA, WA (M. satureioides)
varieties.traits: Clumping perennial groundcover, delicate appearance, small white flowers in summer, 20cm height
varieties.yield_notes: Generous yields when established, best harvested cooler months
varieties.availability: Tucker Bush humid subtropical climates, native plant specialists Queensland

varieties.name: Vietnamese Mint (Laksa Leaf)
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia, particularly Vietnam (Persicaria odorata - NOT true mint, but mint-like)
varieties.traits: Elongated pointed leaves (8cm) with distinctive V-shaped burgundy marking, red-purple stems, 15-30cm high spreading creeper
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific in right conditions, fast colonizer
varieties.availability: Mudbrick Herb Cottage, Bunnings (seasonal), Asian grocers, Yandina Community Gardens region nurseries

varieties.name: Corsican Mint
varieties.origin: Corsica, Sardinia, Montecristo Island, Mediterranean (M. requienii)
varieties.traits: Tiny creeping groundcover, smallest mint, 3-10cm tall, small oval leaves 2-7mm, mauve flowers, carpet-forming
varieties.yield_notes: Low yield but valued for groundcover
varieties.availability: Rare, specialty collectors only

varieties.name: Pennyroyal (European) 🏆
varieties.origin: Europe, North Africa, Middle East (M. pulegium)
varieties.traits: Low growing with downy oval leaves under 1cm, rosy-lilac flowers late summer, creeping habit 10-40cm
varieties.availability: Limited due to toxicity concerns, specialist suppliers

varieties.name: Field Mint (Corn Mint, Wild Mint) 🏆
varieties.origin: Native to temperate Europe and Asia (M. arvensis)
varieties.traits: Variable appearance, purple-tinged stalks, 18-45cm tall, lilac flowers
varieties.availability: Wild-collected, some specialty suppliers

varieties.name: Water Mint (Marsh Mint) 🏆
varieties.origin: Native to Europe, aquatic species (M. aquatica)
varieties.traits: Round to oval leaves, grows in water/wet areas, purple flowers
varieties.availability: Aquatic plant specialists, pond suppliers

varieties.name: Chinese Mint
varieties.origin: China, East Asia (M. arvensis ssp. haplocalyx)
varieties.traits: Similar to field mint, Asian variety
varieties.availability: Asian herb suppliers, specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Lemon Mint (Lemon Bee Balm)
varieties.origin: North America (Monarda citriodora - NOT true Mentha but mint family)
varieties.traits: Similar appearance to other mints, pale purple flowers, square stems, drought-tolerant, 90cm tall
varieties.availability: Specialist seed companies (Survival Garden Seeds type suppliers)

varieties.name: Lemon Balm 🏆
varieties.origin: Mediterranean, Central Europe (Melissa officinalis - mint family but not Mentha)
varieties.traits: Small white flowers, green serrated leaves, bushy 30-60cm
varieties.availability: Widely available - Bunnings, all herb nurseries, common

varieties.name: Catmint/Catnip 🏆
varieties.origin: Europe, Asia (Nepeta cataria - mint family Lamiaceae, not Mentha)
varieties.traits: Gray-green heart-shaped leaves, white-purple flowers, 50-100cm
varieties.availability: Common - Bunnings, all nurseries, widely grown

varieties.name: Japanese Mint - Himalaya (MAS-1)
varieties.origin: Selection released by CIMAP Lucknow, India (M. arvensis)
varieties.traits: Perennial herb, 40-80cm height, quadrangulate branched stem, short tomentose
varieties.yield_notes: 20-25 tonnes/hectare fresh herb from three cuttings, 50-70 kg oil/ha
varieties.availability: CIMAP research stations, commercial menthol growers

varieties.name: Japanese Mint - Kalka (Hyb-77)
varieties.origin: Tall vigorous variety evolved by CIMAP Lucknow
varieties.traits: Tall, vigorous growth habit, robust stems
varieties.availability: CIMAP network, commercial growers

varieties.name: Japanese Mint - Shivalik
varieties.origin: Introduced from China, released by CIMAP Lucknow
varieties.traits: Adapted Chinese variety for Indian conditions
varieties.availability: CIMAP sources

varieties.name: Japanese Mint - Ec-41911
varieties.origin: Progeny selection from interspecific cross M. arvensis × M. piperita
varieties.traits: Hybrid vigor, combines characteristics of both parents
varieties.availability: Research institutions

varieties.name: Japanese Mint - Gomti
varieties.traits: Indian-bred selection for local conditions
varieties.availability: Commercial nurseries North India, CIMAP

varieties.name: Japanese Mint - Sambhav
varieties.traits: Modern variety for commercial cultivation
varieties.availability: CIMAP network

varieties.name: Japanese Mint - Saksham
varieties.traits: Improved disease resistance

varieties.name: Spearmint - Arka Neera
varieties.traits: Developed for Indian growing conditions, dark green serrated leaves
varieties.availability: CIMAP, Indian agricultural universities, tropical monsoon climates nurseries

varieties.name: Spearmint - Ganga
varieties.traits: Adapted to Indian climate
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates agricultural suppliers, local nurseries

varieties.name: Spearmint - Neerkalka
varieties.traits: Vigorous growth, good leaf production
varieties.availability: Indian agricultural institutions

varieties.name: Spearmint - MSS-5
varieties.traits: Further refined selection from MSS-1
varieties.availability: CIMAP network

varieties.name: Spearmint - Punjab Spearmint-1
varieties.origin: Clonal selection made at CIMAP Lucknow
varieties.traits: Selected for uniformity and vigor
varieties.availability: Indian agricultural suppliers

varieties.name: Bergamot Mint - Kiran
varieties.traits: Citrus-scented mint, attractive appearance
varieties.availability: CIMAP, specialty Indian nurseries

varieties.name: Peppermint - Kukrail
varieties.origin: High yielding variety developed and released by CIMAP Lucknow (M. piperita)
varieties.traits: Dark green leaves with purple stems
varieties.availability: CIMAP, Indian agricultural suppliers, tropical monsoon climates hill region nurseries

varieties.name: Peppermint - Prajwal
varieties.traits: Modern variety for Indian conditions
varieties.availability: CIMAP network

varieties.name: Peppermint - Tushar
varieties.traits: Peppermint characteristics
varieties.availability: Research institutions

varieties.name: Peppermint - CIM Indus
varieties.traits: Recent improvement variety

varieties.name: Peppermint - CIM Maduras
varieties.traits: Latest breeding achievement

varieties.name: Common Pudina (Garden Pudina) 🏆 🌏
varieties.origin: Traditional Indian variety, cultivated for centuries (M. spicata/M. arvensis types)
varieties.traits: Green serrated leaves, strong smell, tender leaves, annual shrub 30-60cm

varieties.name: Pahari Pudina (Hill Mint) 🏆 🌏
varieties.origin: Grows in cool hilly climates of India
varieties.traits: Adapted to hill regions, robust growth

varieties.name: Vilayati Pudina (Foreign Mint)
varieties.origin: Introduced variety, likely peppermint or similar
varieties.traits: Distinguished from common pudina, stronger menthol
varieties.availability: Some tropical monsoon climates nurseries, specialty growers

varieties.name: Jungli Pudina (Wild Mint) 🏆 🌏
varieties.traits: Grows wild along streams, moist areas, smaller than cultivated
varieties.availability: Wild-collected, not typically nursery-sold

varieties.name: Ban Pudina (Forest Mint) 🏆 🌏
varieties.traits: Grows in forested areas, shade-tolerant
varieties.availability: Wild forage, forest communities, rare in cultivation

varieties.name: Common Spearmint (Garden Mint) 🏆
varieties.origin: Europe and Asia, cultivated for centuries (M. spicata)
varieties.traits: Dark green serrated crinkled leaves, 30-60cm tall
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates nurseries, agricultural suppliers

varieties.traits: Dark green pointed leaves with purple stems, 60-90cm

varieties.name: Apple Mint (Woolly Mint) 🏆

varieties.name: Vietnamese Mint (Laksa Leaf)

varieties.name: Ginger Mint (Red Mint) 🏆

varieties.availability: Growing availability, specialty suppliers
